docs.text: Welcome to NOMAD Gallery — a space to showcase innovative features, uploads, and use cases created with the NOMAD platform. Whether you want to share your latest work or explore what’s possible, this is the place to engage, discover, and inspire. Featured highlights Hand-picked examples that demonstrate what's possible with NOMAD — from large-scale databases to innovative workflows. The Alexandria Database hosted by the NOMAD Calculations App View in Gallery Search interface for the perovskite solar cell database View in Gallery Visualization of a lipid bilayer according to the coarse-grained MARTINI model View in Gallery Explore the NOMAD Gallery Browse community-submitted use cases showing how NOMAD is used in real research workflows. Use the filters below to explore by methodology, research field, or keywords. Methodology Research field Country Keywords 0 of 0 Sort: Newest Clear Ionic Liquids under shear Max-Planck Institute for Polymer Research • DE Material Science Computational This is a test card to verify the new schema fields. It demonstrates how the Research Field, Institution, Data Size, and Metrics appear on the gallery card. #Ionic Liquids #Molecular Dynamics #Non-equilibrium By Abbas Gholami 2025-12-13 View Details Data Size 1.5 TB Technique MD simulations Usage Statistics 150 active users 5000 downloads Contributors Joe Rudzinski Keywords #Ionic Liquids #Molecular Dynamics #Non-equilibrium Publication Reference https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0279946 Repository Reference https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-gallery Funding Reference DFG - Project No. 460197019 Media URL https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0279946 The Alexandria Database hosted by the NOMAD Calculations App Ruhr-Universität Bochum • DE Materials database Computational The Alexandria database is being ingested into NOMAD! The result is tens of millions of NOMAD calculations entries that can be efficiently queried and explored via NOMAD's extensive search and filtering functionalities. #Materials Database #High-throughput Calculations #PBEsol #PBE By Joseph Rudzinski 2025-02-17 View Details Technique DFT, PBE, PBEsol Contributors Miguel A.L. Marques, Jonathan Schmidt, Hai-Chen Wang, Tiago Cerqueira, Silvana Botti Keywords #Materials Database #High-throughput Calculations #PBEsol #PBE Publication Reference https://alexandria.icams.rub.de/ Visualization of a lipid bilayer according to the coarse-grained MARTINI model • The NOMAD visualizer now has support for non-atomic particles (i.e., particles representing something other than a chemical element). By Joseph Rudzinski 2025-02-15 View Details Keywords Search interface for the perovskite solar cell database Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin • DE Photovoltaics Experimental A search interface for exploring the perovskite solar cell database using materials, composition, and device-property filters to support comparison and analysis of photovoltaic performance. #Perovskites #Solar cells #Energy materials #Materials database By Pepe Marquez 2025-02-19 View Details Data Size 1.5 TB Technique perovskite solar cell fabrication, photovoltaic device characterization Usage Statistics 3000 active users Contributors test Keywords #Perovskites #Solar cells #Energy materials #Materials database Publication Reference https://fairmat-nfdi.github.io/nomad-perovskite-solar-cells-database Repository Reference https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-perovskite-solar-cells-database Funding Reference Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG) - Project 460197019 Pulsed Laser Deposition (PLD) • Pulsed Laser Deposition (PLD) By Pepe Márquez (HU Berlin) or GitHub handle 2025-01-01 View Details Keywords

docs.text: Welcome to NOMAD Gallery — a space to showcase innovative features, uploads, and use cases created with the NOMAD platform. Whether you want to share your latest work or explore what’s possible, this is the place to engage, discover, and inspire. Submit your use case We welcome contributions to the NOMAD Gallery! To submit a new entry, simply fill out the submission form . The form will guide you through the required information to ensure your submission follows the correct format. Once submitted, a team member will review your entry to make sure everything looks good and will take care of publishing it. You will receive a confirmation once your submission has been processed. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out through our Discord server or email us .
